behavior: Perches conspicuously in open country, sallies out to catch flying insects, and vigorously defends its territory; migratory.
field_marks: Clean white throat, breast, and belly
Dark square-tipped tail with a crisp white terminal band
habitat: Open and semi-open habitats with scattered trees, shrubs, or perches, including fields, grasslands, forest edges, orchards, and waterside edges.

sources.title: Eastern Kingbird — All About Birds, Cornell Lab of Ornithology
Eastern Kingbird Life History — All About Birds, Cornell Lab of Ornithology
